hard.next_step: 50% confidence 2nd Jul- Proposal for Zycus has been taken to owner of MMC group- Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary and received a positive response and level of interest. Datuk Nural is now working channels to confirm budget tolerance for all 7 ports. Likely 1-2 weeks for responses (including Northport)- the sense is MMC will look at current proposal we have lodged ($240k at Northport) and factor/optimize across all- including small ports and average $150k per port x 7- this remains to be confirmed Met with Theta Edge - Group CEO on 11th and discussed the proposal in detail 6th Jun Proposal to Owner being planned- likely week of 8th 1st Jun- opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner (Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary) 6th April- Path to close- Agliaan (IT & PM for project) must present 2 prices (in total 3) to new CEO who only needs to see they did some diligence, given it is not going to tender- this is fair albeit a step we did not know about. We are coaching him on the format for comparison to be presented today or tomorrow for final sign off. It remains likely they can sign MSA to a 30th Mar date as there is additional financial benefit (we are offering added 1% per additional Port they bring us into and we close in future- potential upside additional 7 Ports) monitoring this account every 2-4 hourly 30th Mar- Revised proposal required to split implementation into 2 phases- Northport team was away on Hari Raya break and back today. Proposal has limited concessions tied to LOI 31st Mar- and full BOM signed by 7t April- 17th March - Proposal Submitted for S2P 12th Mar Final session complete- internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO) and ck to follow up our champion for close path. 11th Feb - have shared the workflow for the S2P they would like to see, plan to have this workshop in the last week of Feb after CNY 9th Feb-Scope back to S2P- management for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped- meeting on Wed 11th to discuss detail- would like sample contracts to eval. 29th Jan Siti now has approval to move forward with Zycus modules of choice- although mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires 20th Jan- Siti advises we are tracking to get management buy in- Ramco pushing them hard (are aware to P2P side for them at risk) 14th Jan- next step is pricing 5th Jan - Siti (our champion) has asked for costing estimates to present to CFO for (S2C) option and S2P option- in her exco meeting 23rd Jan Ramco have cancelled last 2 meetings and have put their P2P renewal at risk- this is a good opportunity for Zycus to move in, and dominate the MMC group of Ports in Malaysia- ck to drive value prop and aiming for a Q4 result 10th Dec Great f2f with Siti and team. They are strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco. They will bring proposal to management (CEO) who is interested in the procurement manual processes- and Tendering/sourcing 26th Nov Good meeting with Zalekha (Siti) who is keen to see Zycus demo in person. Open to replacing Ramco (Zalekha) to send current tender process flow . Zycus to prepare a customized demo covering tender, RFQ, CLM, and Teams. Zycus to include PTP case study and Ramco integration details. Both teams to propose a date for onsite demo (in coming 2–3 weeks).

north_star_verdict.headline: The direct Northport buyer front has gone quiet: last verified two-way buyer touch was Agilaan's 2026-04-16 reference-visit email, 81 days ago. The deal has since morphed into an MMC-group 7-port consolidation worked entirely through Carl Kimball's back-channel to owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary via Datuk Nural (Next_Step 2026-07-02), with budget tolerance still unconfirmed. In Formal Evaluation at 55 days from a 2026-08-30 close with contracting, SOW, legal and InfoSec all unstarted, the date is not credible; realistic close is Q4 2026.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 55 (close 2026-08-30, today 2026-07-06). Last verified buyer activity 2026-04-16 (81 days cold on the direct buyer front). In Formal Evaluation since creation 2025-11-25 (223 days in stage). Amount rebased 240000 -> 650000 on 2026-06-06 when scope expanded from single Northport S2P to a 7-port MMC consolidation. Next_Step 2026-07-02 shows rep back-channel to the MMC owner awaiting a 1-2 week budget-tolerance response, not buyer engagement. Gates before close: owner sign-off on 7-port budget (pending), Northport CFO/CPO/CIO alignment (internal discussion 2026-03-12, unconfirmed), then LOI/BOM/MSA, SOW scoping, legal redlines and InfoSec (none started). Even with owner approval in 14 days, 90-120 days of contracting and kickoff puts a realistic close in Oct-Nov 2026, not 2026-08-30.
evidence: LastActivityDate 2026-04-16 (81 days cold on the direct Northport buyer front)

Next_Step 2026-07-02: proposal taken to MMC owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary via Datuk Nural, 'positive response and level of interest', budget tolerance for all 7 ports unconfirmed (1-2 week window)
Field history 2026-06-06: Amount 240000 -> 650000 and Stage Shortlisted -> Formal Evaluation as scope expanded to the 7-port MMC play
Stage is Formal Evaluation, not Vendor Selected or Negotiation: buyer has not formally entered contracting
Next_Step 2026-04-06: pricing comparison to be presented to new Northport CEO 'today or tomorrow', status unconfirmed since
Task 2026-04-16: 'Client Reference Visit & Next Steps' email to Agilaan, references still owed
No Avoma call with substantive buyer content since the 2026-02-11 update discussion

metrics.narrative: The pain is clearly named but never quantified. On the 2025-11-25 intro call Nazual Azma Reffin (Head Procurement, speaking as 'Nazwalek') said the tender process 'is doing manually' and called it 'quite urgent now since we're supposed to migrate to capital-ised kind of transaction', and Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus confirmed on 2025-12-30 that spend reporting 'is currently done in Ramco but needs rework'. No baseline was ever captured: no tender cycle-time, no cost-per-tender, no error rate, no headcount or savings target. Siti asked for costing estimates to take to the CFO for S2C and S2P options (Next_Step 2026-01-05), so a value conversation happened internally, but no quantified ROI is on record. With the deal now a 7-port, 650000 consolidation, the missing quantified 7-port savings case is the single biggest hole the owner will test before signing.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits at two levels and neither has been engaged directly by the relevant stakeholder, the CFO controls the sign-off: Siti asked for costing 'to present to CFO' (Next_Step 2026-01-05) and an 'internal discussion with CFO/CIO/CPO' is logged 2026-03-12, but no Northport CFO has appeared on any call and no sign-off is confirmed. For the expanded 7-port play the true economic buyer is Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary, owner of the MMC group, named in Next_Step 2026-07-02 as having received the proposal via the relevant stakeholder with a 'positive response and level of interest', budget tolerance pending. Both are inferred from the Next_Step record, not from a Salesforce contact role, and both are reached only through Carl Kimball's back-channel rather than a direct Zycus relationship, which is the central exposure on this deal.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are well documented from the discovery and workshop-scoping calls. The buyer's priority is end-to-end e-bidding and tender automation that Ramco does not provide: on 2025-11-25 Nazual pressed on 'how do you start with the e bidding from the tender documents, how do you control the e bidding processes, the participating vendors, how do you award' and integration back to Ramco for PO issuance. On 2025-12-30 Siti confirmed the core BOM as eSourcing (catalog/tender), eProcurement, and Merlin Intake as the key requirements, with iRisk scorecards a strong fit ('they need the scorecards, it's a good fit for our iRisk') and iAnalyze/tail-spend flagged as budget-dependent optional. A tender-fee/deposit payment integration was raised as a custom requirement on 2025-11-25 ('we still need some tender deposits to be submitted'). Ramco co-existence, not rip-and-replace, is an explicit criterion.
sources: Avoma BOM call 2025-12-30 (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus)

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is actually running is visible in the call sequence and Next_Step log, and it has escalated upward. It started as a Northport-only evaluation driven by Siti and IT/procurement (demo 2025-12-09, workshop-scoping calls Dec-Feb), moved to internal management buy-in ('they will bring proposal to management/CEO', Next_Step 2025-12-10), then to a pricing comparison Agilaan had to present to the new Northport CEO who 'only needs to see they did some diligence, given it is not going to tender' (Next_Step 2026-04-06). From 2026-06-01 the deal was deliberately lifted to the MMC group so a single 7-port proposition could go directly to the owner (Next_Step 2026-06-01/07-02). It is NOT a tender/RFP: the path is executive endorsement plus owner budget approval, not a formal scoring process, which is why owner access is decisive.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are essentially unstarted and the paper trail is thin. Next_Step history shows a proposal submitted for S2P on 2026-03-17 and a revised two-phase proposal (Phase 1 MYR460k software + MYR1.04m implementation; Phase 2 MYR480k + MYR560k) sent 2026-03-27, with an LOI targeted 2026-03-31 and full BOM sign-off 2026-04-07 tied to a possible 30-Mar MSA date. Those milestones appear to have lapsed once the deal was re-scoped to the 7-port consolidation. No MSA, Order Form, or SOW is confirmed as drafted or in redline, and there is no evidence yet of legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec/DPA activity. Because this is a Malaysian (APAC) buyer, a PO will likely gate invoicing, but that is far downstream. What would create visibility is a written mutual close plan naming the SOW, legal, and InfoSec owners once the owner budget decision lands.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is confirmed and owned by procurement and tender management. Nazual Azma Reffin stated the tender process 'is doing manually' and framed digitisation as urgent for the migration to capitalised transactions (2025-11-25); Siti described a manual, Excel-driven sourcing ecosystem and a spend-reporting function that 'needs rework' in Ramco (2025-12-30). A recurring pain is Ramco's poor fit for their tender and e-bidding needs and its practice of forcing risk assessment at PO level, which Siti said 'is not suited for their business' (2025-12-30). The pain owners are Siti (tender/contracts) and Nazual (procurement); the inferred financial pain is manual-process cost and compliance/auditability risk, but it has not been sized.
sources: Avoma BOM call 2025-12-30 (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus)

champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) is the champion and her advocacy is well-evidenced historically: she personally invited Zycus in over the incumbent's channel ('that's why we call you personally to present your own products to us instead of having iRequest in', 2025-11-25), drove the demo and BOM scoping, secured internal approval to move on Zycus modules (Next_Step 2026-01-29), and was described as 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (Next_Step 2025-12-10). She reaches the CFO (took costing to the exco). Her weakness now is engagement: she has not been on a call since early 2026 and the champion running the day-to-day since is Agilaan Praemkumar (Lead, Digital Transformation), who received the proposals and had to present pricing to the new CEO. With the play now at MMC-owner level, neither Siti nor Agilaan controls the decision, so the champion is real but no longer positioned at the point of sign-off.
sources: Avoma intro call 2025-11-25 (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus)

competition.narrative: The live competitive dynamic is against the incumbent Ramco, which runs Northport's P2P and S2P today but is being displaced on the tender/e-bidding layer the buyer cares about. The buyer is openly dissatisfied ('strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco', Next_Step 2025-12-10) and Ramco 'cancelled last 2 meetings and put their P2P renewal at risk' (Next_Step ~Jan 2026), though management chose to retain Ramco P2P short-term while its contract expires (Next_Step 2026-01-29). Zycus's wedge is an upstream S2P/e-bidding layer that integrates with the Ramco backend rather than ripping it out (Akshay, 2025-11-25: 'we've got APIs to connect to Ramco'). iValua and SAP Ariba sit on the Salesforce competitor field but are named in no call and are dormant. The real current threat is inertia: budget-constrained, manual-today, and a slow owner-level decision.

deal_movement.summary: The deal cooled on the direct Northport front after the 2026-03 proposals, then was deliberately relaunched at MMC-group scale in June: amount rebased 240000 -> 650000, stage moved to Formal Evaluation, and the proposition is now being carried to owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary via a back-channel. Last verified two-way buyer touch remains 2026-04-16.

items.change: Revised two-phase Zycus proposal sent to Agilaan (Phase 1 MYR460k software + MYR1.04m implementation; Phase 2 MYR480k + MYR560k)
Client reference visit and next-steps email sent to Agilaan; references still owed
Deal re-scoped from single Northport S2P to a 7-port MMC consolidation; CloseDate 2026-06-30 -> 2026-08-30
Amount 240000 -> 650000; Stage Shortlisted -> Formal Evaluation as the 7-port play was formalised
Proposal carried to MMC owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary via Datuk Nural; positive interest, budget tolerance across 7 ports pending

competitive_position.summary: Ranked by current threat: (1) Do-nothing / inertia is the strongest live threat, a budget-constrained buyer running manual processes today with a slow owner-level decision and 81 days of direct silence; (2) Ramco is the incumbent ERP/P2P being displaced on the tender/e-bidding layer, weakened by buyer dissatisfaction and cancelled meetings (Next_Step ~Jan 2026) but retained on P2P short-term while its contract expires; (3) iValua and (4) SAP Ariba appear only on the Salesforce competitor field with no call evidence and are dormant. Zycus's wedge is upstream S2P/e-bidding that integrates with Ramco rather than replacing it, plus the AI/Merlin intake story.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Inertia
competitors.quote: at the moment it's kicking the tire in the market, but in terms of urgency I think it's urgent (Nazual, 2025-11-25); budget constraint kept surfacing on the BOM (Siti, 2025-12-30)
competitors.how_we_win: Force a decision clock: put a quantified 7-port ROI in front of the owner and re-confirm the budget-tolerance response within the stated 1-2 week window.

competitors.name: Ramco (incumbent ERP/P2P)
competitors.quote: strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco (Next_Step 2025-12-10); Ramco is trying to force them to do the risk assessment at a purchase order level, it is not suited for their business (Siti, 2025-12-30)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the upstream S2P/e-bidding layer that integrates with Ramco via API, so the buyer modernises tender/sourcing without a rip-and-replace of P2P.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer evidence iValua is engaged; keep it dormant and lead with the Ramco-integration and AI/Merlin differentiation if it resurfaces.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: No buyer evidence Ariba is in the evaluation; differentiate on single-code-line AI (Merlin) and lower-disruption Ramco co-existence if it appears.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit on tender/e-bidding automation and Ramco integration; the exposure is commercial (7-port budget tolerance) and executive access to the owner, not product.

they loved Anna, but they kept saying budget... that's my concern

items.deliverable: Confirm 7-port budget tolerance and owner sign-off from the MMC group

items.deliverable: Present the Zycus pricing comparison to the new Northport CEO

LOI/BOM milestones from March lapsed when scope expanded; no contracting evidence since

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage 7-port play with strong fit but a cold direct buyer front and no quantified value case; the win levers are owner access, a sized ROI, and re-activating the champion.
flags: Land a quantified 7-port ROI (savings, tender cycle-time, compliance) so the owner's budget-tolerance decision has a defensible number to approve.
Re-open the direct Northport channel: George John to reconnect with Agilaan and Siti, who have been silent 81 days while the owner play runs through a back-channel.
Convert the back-channel to owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary into a direct exec-to-exec touch via Carl Kimball, so the decision does not rest solely on Datuk Nural relaying the proposal.

items.name: Nazual Azma Reffin
items.title: Head Procurement
items.risk: Primary contact-role decision maker who named the pain and urgency, but has not re-engaged on a call since the 2025-11-25 intro.

items.name: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus
items.title: Head of Tender and Contracts
items.risk: The core advocate who invited Zycus in and secured internal approval, now quiet since early 2026 and no longer at the owner-level point of sign-off.

items.name: Agilaan Praemkumar
items.title: Lead, Digital Transformation
items.risk: Day-to-day project owner who receives proposals and presents pricing internally; last engaged 2026-04-16, owes references follow-up and the CEO pricing comparison.

items.name: Aimi Syazreen Ab Murad
items.title: Tender Executive
items.risk: Tender-team influencer on the contact roster; not yet an active thread.

champion_strength.summary: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) is a genuine, historically strong champion who invited Zycus in over the incumbent, drove the demo and BOM scoping, and secured internal approval, with access to the CFO. But she has been quiet since early 2026, the operational baton has passed to Agilaan Praemkumar, and the decision has moved above both of them to the MMC owner, so no active champion sits at the point of sign-off today.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is Strong (8.0) with high AI Readiness (8.8), and the buyer engaged directly on Merlin and agentic AI (Nazual asked how Merlin intake works inside Microsoft Teams on 2025-11-25). The Merlin intake and AI contract-authoring story is landing but has not yet been tied to a quantified outcome for the owner decision.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 8.0 / Strong with high AI Readiness and Enterprise Transformation Momentum; calls corroborate genuine appetite (buyer asked how Merlin agentic AI and intake work in Teams, 2025-11-25), so the field is not over-rated.

items.detail: 7-port budget tolerance unconfirmed; the 150000-per-port x 7 average is a Zycus assumption not yet validated by the owner.
The ultimate economic buyer (MMC owner) is engaged only through a back-channel via the relevant stakeholder; no direct Zycus relationship exists.
Close 2026-08-30 is indefensible: contracting, SOW, legal and InfoSec are unstarted and the direct buyer front has been silent 81 days.

The 7-port consolidation needs an MMC-level strategic sponsor to push it across ports; Siti doubted one exists ('does the MMC have a strategic person who can push that kind of ecosystem through... my guess is they don't', 2025-12-30).

confidence_signals.summary: Direct buyer engagement has cooled to zero for 81 days while the deal is worked entirely through a rep back-channel at owner level; strong historical fit and anti-incumbent sentiment keep it alive, but momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-pulled.
items: Last verified two-way buyer touch 2026-04-16 (81 days)
Next_Step 2026-07-02 is rep back-channel awaiting a buyer reply, not buyer engagement
Scope and amount expanded 2026-06-06 but on the rep's initiative, not a buyer-signed commitment

scope_change.from: Single Northport S2P proposal at ~240000 (MYR pricing, phased)
scope_change.to: MMC-group 7-port consolidation at 650000 taken directly to the owner
scope_change.detail: Field history 2026-06-06 shows Amount 240000 -> 650000 and Stage Shortlisted -> Formal Evaluation as the deal was deliberately lifted from a single-port S2P sale to a 7-port group play (Next_Step 2026-06-01). This is a strategic broadening, not a defensive narrowing, though it trades a near-term single-port close for a slower owner-level decision.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Strong functional fit and an anti-incumbent buyer, but the deal has been deliberately re-aimed at a 7-port MMC-group owner decision that runs entirely through a back-channel while the direct Northport buyer front has been silent 81 days; the win turns on owner budget sign-off and a quantified value case, neither of which exists yet.

win_position.text: We are the buyer-invited alternative to a disliked incumbent: Northport called Zycus in directly over Ramco/iRequest and were 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (Next_Step 2025-12-10), with strong AIS (8.0) fit on tender automation and Merlin intake.

The direct buyer relationship has gone cold: the last two-way touch was Agilaan's 2026-04-16 reference email, and everything since is Carl Kimball's back-channel to the MMC owner rather than buyer-pulled engagement.
There is no quantified 7-port ROI and no confirmed budget tolerance (Next_Step 2026-07-02), so the owner is being asked to approve 650000 on interest, not a defensible value case.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-driven: the June re-scope to 650000 and the owner proposal were initiated by Zycus (Carl Kimball), with the buyer's budget-tolerance response still awaited from a 2026-07-02 back-channel.
No Avoma call has carried substantive buyer content since 2026-02-11, and the March LOI/BOM milestones (targeted 2026-03-31 / 2026-04-07) lapsed when scope expanded.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer secured internal management approval to move on Zycus modules (Next_Step 2026-01-29) and championed us to their CFO and new CEO (pricing comparison, Next_Step 2026-04-06).
Commitment has not converted to paper: still in Formal Evaluation, no signed LOI/MSA, and the champion Siti has gone quiet with the decision now above her at owner level.

deal_risk.text: Single biggest risk is inertia plus indirect access: a budget-constrained buyer, manual today, deciding slowly through a relayed owner proposal with no direct Zycus relationship to the economic buyer.
The 2026-08-30 close is not credible with contracting, SOW, legal and InfoSec all unstarted; realistic close is Q4 2026.

buyer_engagement.evidence: 81 days since last two-way buyer touch (2026-04-16); recent activity is rep back-channel only (Next_Step 2026-07-02).

economic_buyer_access.evidence: MMC owner reached only via Datuk Nural; Northport CFO never on a call.

value_case.evidence: No quantified 7-port ROI or savings baseline on any call or field.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer was 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (Next_Step 2025-12-10) and invited Zycus in directly over the incumbent, but that preference is now decaying with 81 days of direct silence.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: manual tender/procurement processes and Ramco spend-reporting that 'needs rework' (Siti, 2025-12-30); no quantified cycle-time, cost, or savings target captured.

expansion_context.note: No prior Closed-Won on NORTHPORT (MALAYSIA) BHD. PTP is a sibling MMC-group port and an existing Zycus customer used as a reference, but it is a different account, so no inherited executive access applies to this deal.

items.action: Reconnect directly with Agilaan and Siti to confirm Northport's status and the owner budget timeline.
items.trigger: 81 days since the last two-way buyer touch; owner budget-tolerance response due within 1-2 weeks (Next_Step 2026-07-02).
items.expected_effect: Restores a live buyer channel so the owner decision is not the only signal keeping the deal warm.

items.action: Build and send a quantified 7-port ROI model to underpin the owner's consolidation budget decision.
items.trigger: Owner asked to approve 650000 across 7 ports with no quantified value case on record.
items.expected_effect: Gives the owner a defensible number to approve and counters the budget-constraint and inertia risk.

items.action: Deliver the client reference calls and site visit Agilaan requested and left open.
items.trigger: Reference request email to Agilaan still unfulfilled (Task 2026-04-16).
items.expected_effect: Closes an outstanding buyer ask and gives the champion proof points to carry upward.

items.action: Set up a direct exec-to-exec touch from Carl Kimball to the MMC owner alongside the Datuk Nural channel.
items.trigger: Owner proposal is relayed only via the relevant stakeholder, leaving no direct Zycus relationship to the economic buyer.
items.expected_effect: De-risks the sign-off by putting a Zycus leader in front of the decision-maker rather than relying on a relay.

items.action: Co-author a mutual close plan with a realistic Q4 date and named contracting owners.
items.trigger: Close 2026-08-30 is indefensible with SOW, legal and InfoSec unstarted.
items.expected_effect: Rebaselines the forecast honestly and sequences the contracting gates so the date stops slipping silently.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 35 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 19 is anchored on the stage win (35), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: NORTHPORT (MALAYSIA) BHD — Formal Evaluation · $650K · closes 30 Aug
cro_panel.intro: Strong functional fit and an anti-incumbent buyer, but the deal has been deliberately re-aimed at a 7-port MMC-group owner decision that runs entirely through a back-channel while the direct Northport buyer front has been silent 81 days; the win turns on owner budget sign-off and a quantified value case, neither of which exists yet

bullets.text: We are the buyer-invited alternative to a disliked incumbent; Northport called Zycus in directly over Ramco/iRequest and were 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti Ramco' (Next_Step 2025-12-10), with strong AIS (8.0) fit on tender automation and Merlin intake

The direct buyer relationship has gone cold; the last two-way touch was Agilaan's 2026-04-16 reference email, and everything since is Carl Kimball's back-channel to the MMC owner rather than buyer-pulled engagement
There is no quantified 7-port ROI and no confirmed budget tolerance (Next_Step 2026-07-02), so the owner is being asked to approve 650000 on interest, not a defensible value case

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum is rep-driven, not buyer-driven; the June re-scope to 650000 and the owner proposal were initiated by Zycus (Carl Kimball), with the buyer's budget-tolerance response still awaited from a 2026-07-02 back-channel
No Avoma call has carried substantive buyer content since 2026-02-11, and the March LOI/BOM milestones (targeted 2026-03-31 / 2026-04-07) lapsed when scope expanded

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Single biggest risk is inertia plus indirect access; a budget-constrained buyer, manual today, deciding slowly through a relayed owner proposal with no direct Zycus relationship to the economic buyer
The 2026-08-30 close is not credible with contracting, SOW, legal and InfoSec all unstarted; realistic close is Q4 2026

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Ranked by current threat: (1) Do-nothing / inertia is the strongest live threat, a budget-constrained buyer running manual processes today with a slow owner-level decision and 81 days of direct silence; (2) Ramco is the incumbent ERP/P2P…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: The buyer secured internal management approval to move on Zycus modules (Next_Step 2026-01-29) and championed us to their CFO and new CEO (pricing comparison, Next_Step 2026-04-06)
Commitment has not converted to paper; still in Formal Evaluation, no signed LOI/MSA, and the champion Siti has gone quiet with the decision now above her at owner level

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Reconnect directly with Agilaan and Siti to confirm Northport's status and the owner budget timeline. (by 10 Jul)
Build and send a quantified 7-port ROI model to underpin the owner's consolidation budget decision. (by 17 Jul)
Deliver the client reference calls and site visit Agilaan requested and left open. (by 18 Jul)

calls_omitted.reason: recorded but transcript is audio-setup chatter only, no substantive content

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities 120d; field history full; Next_Step_History from creation 2025-11-25
gaps: No quantified 7-port business case / ROI baseline in any source
Economic buyers (MMC owner Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary; Northport CFO) never engaged on a call; reached only via back-channel / internal discussion
Contracting artifacts (SOW, MSA, legal, InfoSec) absent since the March LOI/BOM milestones lapsed
Two most recent meetings (2026-03-26, 2026-03-09) carry no usable transcript, so the freshest direct-voice evidence is 2026-02-11
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Datuk Nural')
